seoTitle: Long Flat vs Long Jetty — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Long Flat and Long Jetty. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Long Flat vs Long Jetty: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Long Flat and Long Jetty.
heading: Long Flat vs Long Jetty — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Long Flat, New South Wales and Long Jetty, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Long Jetty has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Long Flat (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Long Flat or Long Jetty safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Long Flat has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Long Jetty scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Long Flat scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Long Jetty scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Long Flat or Long Jetty?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Long Jetty has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
